Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **1/10** (calm price action, no signals firing) vs VIX Spike Tier **2/4** MEDIUM with **60%** probability, skew and backwardation both active
Realized vol and breadth say nothing is breaking, but the options surface is paying up for downside and the front of the VIX curve is inverted. Trust the VIX tier here. The Crash Index measures what already happened; the vol surface is forward-looking and dealers rarely pay this much for tails without a reason.
⚠ These two disagree: Regime State **14** STABLE with no regime shift vs Divergence composite ELEVATED at **57.5%** of pairs diverging, Mother Jaws **100**
Regime score reads the level, divergences read the plumbing underneath. When over half of cross-asset pairs are drifting out of their usual correlation while the headline regime says STABLE, trust the divergences. Correlation breaks lead regime shifts, they don't follow them.
⚠ These two disagree: Macro Regime BROADENING (bullish rotation signal) vs Macro Regime confidence only **30%**
The label says rotation is widening out, but confidence sits in the bottom third. Trust the low confidence. A BROADENING call at **30%** conviction is a hypothesis, not a trade. Size accordingly.
Today's tension
Calm price action sits on top of an options market quietly pricing a vol expansion.
Bull
The Crash Index reads **1/10**, the Regime State scores **14** as STABLE, and breadth is broadening across regions with Korea **+14.7%** and the STOXX 50 **+4.8%** on a 5-day basis. Nothing in the realized tape says stop.
Bear
VIX Spike Detector sits at tier **2/4** with skew and term-structure backwardation both active, and **57.5%** of cross-asset pairs are diverging in the ELEVATED zone. Options dealers are paying up for protection while spot drifts.
The tell: Watch the VIX Spike probability: it sits at **60%**. If the adaptive flag also fires while the Crash Index stays low, the options market is right and you fade the calm.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and uneasy underneath. Crash Index reads 1/10, Regime State scores 14 STABLE, and the Contrarian gauge sits at 45 ACCUMULATE on a partial signal. At the same time the VIX Spike Detector is at tier 2/4 MEDIUM with a 60% probability print, both skew and term-structure backwardation are active, and 57.5% of the 40 monitored cross-asset pairs are diverging with 19 bearish to 4 bullish. That is the central tension: price action and breadth say stay long, the options surface and the correlation plumbing say buy protection.

For positioning you stay in the trade but stop adding. The BROADENING macro call at 30% confidence is too thin to chase, and the Contrarian read of 45 is not deep enough to buy weakness aggressively. Hold core US and global exposure, harvest premium against it sparingly, and rotate marginal dollars from leadership (NDX +4.1% on 5d) toward laggards where dispersion has opened up. Use the cheap part of the vol surface to own tails rather than selling them. The VIX backwardation flag is the cleanest hedge tell on the page.

The risk is that the divergences resolve down before the Crash Index ever lights up. The single reading that flips you defensive is the VIX Spike adaptive flag turning on while the Crash Index stays low. That combination would mean dealers are right and the calm is borrowed. The reading that confirms the bull case is the divergence composite falling out of ELEVATED while macro regime confidence rises above 50%.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence. Translated: leadership is trying to widen out from the megacap concentration of the last leg, but the model is not yet convinced the rotation is real.

A 30% print sits in the bottom third of conviction, which means you treat the call as a tilt rather than a thesis. Position by holding your core index exposure and adding selectively to laggards and equal-weight expressions where the divergence composite shows correlation snapping toward +0.88 between SPY and RSP.

Do not pay up for rotation themes. The signal that promotes this from tilt to trade is regime confidence rising above 50% while the divergence zone drops out of ELEVATED.

Until then, lean long quality with a small barbell into broadening beneficiaries.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The cross-asset wiring is loosening. Divergence means two assets that usually move together are starting to move apart, and correlation drift tracks how fast that relationship is changing.

The composite sits in the ELEVATED zone with 57.5% of 40 pairs diverging, 19 of them bearish against just 4 bullish, and the Mother Jaws score pegs 100. Look at the specific breaks: SPY-RSP correlation drifted from +0.12 to +0.88 (the cap-weighted index and the equal-weighted version are now joined at the hip, which usually marks a narrow tape getting forced into rotation), LQD-IEF drifted from -0.02 to +0.85 (credit and duration are trading as one risk factor again), and the 2s10s curve flipped from +0.56 to -0.54 correlation with its driver.

The standout is STLFSI4 vs the 20-day composite at score 73 DEEP DIVERG, drifting from +0.39 to -0.05: financial stress and the broad risk read have decoupled. When that many pairs break at once, the next move is usually a regime change in either direction.

You respect the warning by trimming convexity-short trades.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of the three monitored confluences are active today. VIX backwardation is not pairing with HYG credit stress, not pairing with small-cap B2 drawdown, and not pairing with JPY safe-haven flow.

That is the single most bullish line on the page. The vol surface is twitching on its own, but the corroborating risk-off pillars (credit widening, small caps breaking, yen bid) are all asleep.

You read this as a one-factor warning, not a system warning. Lean: stay long risk, treat the VIX signal as a hedging tell rather than a directional sell, and only escalate to defensive when at least one of HYG, B2, or JPY joins the backwardation print.

Until then, the absence of confluence is permission to keep your exposure on.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

Asia is doing the heavy lifting and the US is being pulled along. Korea ran +14.7% over 5 days as the regional leader while Hong Kong lagged at -0.4%, a dispersion that says the bid is selective and tech-heavy rather than a broad Asia melt-up.

Europe confirms the risk-on tone with the STOXX 50 +4.8% and even the laggard FTSE up +2.5%, which is a cleaner broadening read than Asia. The US shows NDX +4.1% leading the SPX at +2.1%, so megacap tech is still doing the work at home and breadth has not caught up.

Crypto is the dissent: ETH only +1.4% and SOL -2.7% mean the 24/7 risk proxy is not validating the equity bid. For today's US session, the read is constructive into the open on the Asia and Europe handoff, but you fade strength in tech-heavy leadership if crypto stays soft through the morning.

The lead-lag wants rotation into laggards, not more concentration into what already worked.

01
Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 1/10 · WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index 1/10 and VIX Spike tier 2/4 disagreeing is a coherent state, not a bug. The Crash Index reads what already happened in price: realized drawdown, breadth breaks, credit cracks.

None of that is firing, so the score is 1. The VIX Spike Detector reads what the options market is paying for the next move: skew is active (puts bid relative to calls), term-structure backwardation is active (front VIX above the back, meaning near-term fear above longer-dated fear), and the model spits out a 60% spike probability.

Dealers are hedging while spot drifts. You see this setup before vol events, not during them.

The right read is to trust the forward-looking signal: own gamma cheaply now, because by the time the Crash Index moves off 1 the hedges will cost three times as much. The adaptive flag is still off, so this is a warning, not a panic.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 1/10, so the tape is calm and there is no crash setup.
CRASH INDEX
1/10

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Probability VIX +50% within 60d
60%
vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (0)
none
TOPPING aligned (1)
• SMH stretched extreme
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
